full_name: Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act

rules.description: All Protected Health Information (PHI) must be encrypted both at rest and in transit using AES-256 or equivalent.
rules.fix_guidance: Encrypt all PHI fields using AES-256. Use TLS 1.2+ for data in transit. Implement field-level encryption for sensitive columns.

rules.description: Implement role-based access controls (RBAC) to limit PHI access to authorized personnel only.
rules.fix_guidance: Implement RBAC middleware. Require authentication for all PHI endpoints. Use least-privilege access principle.

rules.description: All access to PHI must be logged with who accessed what, when, and from where. Logs must be retained for 6 years.
rules.fix_guidance: Implement audit logging middleware that records: user ID, timestamp, action, resource accessed, IP address. Use append-only log storage.

rules.rule: No PHI in logs or error messages
rules.description: Protected Health Information must never appear in application logs, error messages, console output, or monitoring dashboards.
rules.fix_guidance: Sanitize all log outputs to remove PHI. Use structured logging with PHI field redaction. Log only de-identified data.

rules.description: Any third-party service handling PHI must have a signed Business Associate Agreement (BAA).
rules.fix_guidance: Ensure BAAs are signed with all cloud providers and third-party services that process PHI. Use HIPAA-eligible service tiers.

rules.description: Only the minimum amount of PHI necessary for the intended purpose should be collected, used, or disclosed.
rules.fix_guidance: Select only required columns instead of SELECT *. Validate that only necessary PHI fields are collected in forms.
